scores.justification: Methodology is rigorous and claims are carefully calibrated to evidence (pre-registered tests, power floors, post-hoc flags at every mention); one specific weakness — the class-level trainability comparison is ordering-only and its metric-robustness margin (0.001) inverts on the current committed artifact — does not undermine the headline phase-order claim.

Readable but rough in places: five severe overfull hboxes push artifact paths into the margin, the Figure 1 / Table 1 captions mischaracterize the unsampled cells, and the one-page abstract impedes first-pass extraction.

Solid but incremental for the Cooperative AI community: an NE-transparent parametric benchmark plus two honestly-retired predictors and a measured social-trap headroom target is useful methodological infrastructure, though the niche (equilibrium-convergence quality on a small custom game) is narrow by design.

The parametric phase-diagram-with-computable-NE framing is new, but the closest prior tradition (OpenSpiel small games with exact solvers; exploitability/NashConv-based convergence evaluation) is omitted from the related-work section, leaving the 'only published' novelty claim under-qualified.

Methods, seeds, hyperparameter pins, frozen scenario IDs, and a parity CLI are supplied and the repo artifacts exist; held below full because the cited recalibrated_verdict.json has drifted from the paper's numbers (n=20 regeneration) and the baseline-checkpoint hosting is still in progress.

findings.rationale: prior_work_omission risk (advisory): OpenSpiel and the exploitability-evaluation literature are the reference points a NeurIPS reviewer will reach for against the 'only published parametric MARL game' claim.
findings.suggested_fix: Engage that tradition explicitly or scope the claim.

findings.rationale: The NeurIPS checklist expects the hosted-baselines pathway to be resolved, not 'in progress'.
findings.suggested_fix: Complete or remove the HuggingFace hosting promise before submission.
